findings.message: Link text reads "thought circulation" but URL anchor is #signatures and the documented term is "thought signatures" (SDK exposes signature field, referenced in gemini-interactions-api/SKILL.md line 246)
"some" used without measurable criterion in describing knowledge gap
"relevant context" used without measurable criterion

Section "When MCP is NOT Installed (Fallback Only)" instructs the agent to "Use fetch_url to:" — backticked as a tool name; sibling skill gemini-live-api-dev/SKILL.md line 266 uses agent-neutral phrasing "Use web fetch tools to:" which works across Claude Code (WebFetch), Cursor, Codex CLI, and other host agents
"relevant" used in MCP fallback section without measurable criterion
"relevant" used in MCP fallback section without measurable criterion
Two consecutive list items both numbered "2." in the MCP documentation lookup section; second step is effectively skipped by an agent that processes numbered steps strictly in order
"relevant" used in MCP fallback section without measurable criterion

"Nano Banana Pro" originally flagged as placeholder text; verification confirmed it is the official Google codename/marketing name for the gemini-3-pro-image-preview model family (https://blog.google/technology/ai/nano-banana-pro/)

"Nano Banana" originally flagged as placeholder text; verification confirmed it is the official Google codename/marketing name for the gemini-2.5-flash-image model family (https://blog.google/products-and-platforms/products/gemini/how-nano-banana-got-its-name/)

Vertex skill names the documentation MCP tool search_documents; gemini-api-dev, gemini-live-api-dev, and gemini-interactions-api all name it search_documentation; an agent loading both receives different tool names for what reads like the same operation

Vertex skill lists gemini-live-2.5-flash-native-audio as the primary Live model; gemini-live-api-dev/SKILL.md line 29 marks the 2.5 family deprecated and designates gemini-3.1-flash-live-preview as the sole recommended Live model
"relevant" used in MCP fallback section without measurable criterion

Instructional MCP example uses StdioServerParameters(command='npx', args=['-y', '@philschmid/weather-mcp']); -y silently accepts the latest published version of a third-party npm package not maintained by Google; reproduced verbatim, a supply-chain compromise of that package would execute in user environments
Uses deprecated Live model gemini-live-2.5-flash-native-audio; gemini-live-api-dev/SKILL.md marks this family deprecated and directs migration to gemini-3.1-flash-live-preview

send_client_content used to send a mid-session user message; gemini-live-api-dev/SKILL.md line 62 states this method is only supported for seeding initial context history and must not be used for new user messages
send_realtime_input(media=Blob(...)) uses the media key; gemini-live-api-dev/SKILL.md lines 65-66 explicitly prohibit this and require specific keys (audio/video/text)
Example uses media=Blob(...) key explicitly prohibited by gemini-live-api-dev/SKILL.md line 65; doc consistency defect with no security impact
"sufficient" used without measurable quality threshold
Variable model_id passed to client.models.generate_content(model=model_id, ...) is never defined in the snippet; reproducing this code raises NameError at runtime

"Nano Banana Pro" originally flagged as placeholder text; verification confirmed it is the official Google codename/marketing name for the gemini-3-pro-image-preview model family (https://blog.google/technology/ai/nano-banana-pro/)

"Nano Banana" originally flagged as placeholder text; verification confirmed it is the official Google codename/marketing name for the gemini-2.5-flash-image model family (https://blog.google/products-and-platforms/products/gemini/how-nano-banana-got-its-name/)
Uses deprecated Live model gemini-live-2.5-flash-native-audio; gemini-live-api-dev/SKILL.md marks this family deprecated and directs migration to gemini-3.1-flash-live-preview

send_client_content used to send a mid-session user message; gemini-live-api-dev/SKILL.md line 62 states this method is only supported for seeding initial context history and must not be used for new user messages
send_realtime_input(media=Blob(...)) uses the media key; gemini-live-api-dev/SKILL.md lines 65-66 explicitly prohibit this and require specific keys (audio/video/text)
Variable model_id passed to client.models.generate_content(model=model_id, ...) is never defined in the snippet; reproducing this code raises NameError at runtime

Section "When MCP is NOT Installed (Fallback Only)" instructs the agent to "Use fetch_url to:" — backticked as a tool name; sibling skill gemini-live-api-dev/SKILL.md line 266 uses agent-neutral phrasing "Use web fetch tools to:" which works across Claude Code (WebFetch), Cursor, Codex CLI, and other host agents
